_**User**_

Read ALL blog posts in /home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com/content/english/blog/*.md (exclude _index.md and __blog-template.md).

For each post, assess "difficulty of understanding" based on:
- Prerequisite knowledge required (technical jargon, domain expertise)
- Conceptual complexity (abstract ideas vs concrete how-to)
- Writing clarity (plain language helps even hard topics)
- Length and density of information
- Whether reader needs to follow multi-step technical workflows

Return a structured ranking from EASIEST to HARDEST with:
1. Title
2. slug/filename
3. draft: true/false
4. Brief 1-sentence justification for difficulty level
5. Suggested difficulty tier (1=effortless, 2=easy, 3=moderate, 4=hard, 5=expert)

Group by tier. Include ALL posts (~58). Be consistent. Published posts matter most but include drafts too.

Read enough of each file to judge fairly - at least frontmatter + substantial body for short posts, skim long ones.

---

# Blog Post Difficulty Ranking (Easiest → Hardest)

**Scope:** 58 posts in `/home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com/content/english/blog/` (excluding `_index.md` and `__blog-template.md`)

**Criteria applied consistently:** prerequisite jargon, conceptual abstraction, writing clarity (JoRap’s plain voice lowers difficulty even on technical topics), length/density, and multi-step technical workflows.

**Counts:** Tier 1: 13 · Tier 2: 19 · Tier 3: 15 · Tier 4: 10 · Tier 5: 1

---

## Tier 1 — Effortless
*No special knowledge; read and done.*

| Title | Slug / filename | Draft | Justification |
|---|---|---|---|
| Blog Template | `how-to-create-a-blog-template` / `__how-to-create-a-blog-template.md` | true | Empty body beyond frontmatter — nothing to parse. |
| Interesting Facts About JoRap | `interesting-facts-about-jorap` / `__interesting-facts-about-jorap.md` | true | Meta index of personal facts in tables; no concepts to learn. |
| DNPAP - Song Resources | `dnpap-song-resources` / `dnpap-song-resources.md` | false | Link/embed hub with a short personal intro; no workflow or jargon. |
| The Miracle of Isaiah David | `miracle-isaiah-david` / `miracle-isaiah-david.md` | false | Straightforward birth testimony told chronologically in plain language. |
| Worcestershire Sauce - Lee and Perrins | `worcestershire-sauce` / `__worcestershire-sauce.md` | true | Pantry review with everyday cooking uses anyone can follow. |
| Kewpie Mayo | `kewpie-mayo` / `__kewpie-mayo.md` | true | Condiment comparison and usage tips; zero technical or abstract load. |
| Why I Stopped Playing Marvel Snap | `why-i-stopped-playing-marvel-snap` / `why-i-stopped-playing-marvel-snap.md` | false | Personal gaming essay; game terms are explained in context. |
| Facebook's Hidden Gem: The Favorites Feed | `facebook-favorites-feed` / `facebook-favorites-feed.md` | false | Short UI walkthrough with numbered steps and screenshots. |
| The Set It and Forget It Arroz Caldo | `set-it-forget-it-arroz-caldo` / `set-it-forget-it-arroz-caldo.md` | false | Simple rice-cooker recipe with linear prep steps. |
| The Short Extension Cord in My Laptop Bag | `extension-cord` / `extension-cord.md` | false | Concrete gear advice in short sections; no domain expertise needed. |
| The Mouse Wheel Click | `mouse-wheel-click` / `mouse-wheel-click.md` | false | One-gesture productivity tip repeated across familiar apps. |
| Rechargeable Batteries | `rechargeable-batteries` / `__rechargeable-batteries.md` | true | Household gear checklist with a simple use/don’t-use table. |
| Remote Work Checklist | `remote-work-checklist` / `__remote-work-checklist.md` | true | Bag-packing table with one-line reasons; no workflow to execute. |

---

## Tier 2 — Easy
*Light context or a single how-to; jargon minimal or explained.*

| Title | Slug / filename | Draft | Justification |
|---|---|---|---|
| Instant Pot Arroz Caldo | `instant-pot-arroz-caldo` / `instant-pot-arroz-caldo.md` | false | Recipe with a few Instant Pot rules (natural release, seasoning) but still linear cooking. |
| Instant Pot Chicken Adobo | `instant-pot-chicken-adobo` / `instant-pot-chicken-adobo.md` | false | Detailed pressure-cook recipe; needs basic Instant Pot literacy but no abstract concepts. |
| Worship Pads for Solo Guitar | `worship-pads-solo-guitar` / `worship-pads-solo-guitar.md` | false | Explains “pads” from scratch; practical MP3/VLC setup, mild worship-context assumed. |
| Smart Plug | `smart-plug` / `__smart-plug.md` | true | Consumer IoT review; ecosystem lock-in is the only mild complexity. |
| Mi Box S | `mi-box-s` / `__mi-box-s.md` | true | Streaming-box pros/cons; needs basic “Android TV box” context. |
| Canon G3000 | `canon-g3000` / `__canon-g3000.md` | true | Ink-tank printer review with cost/maintenance tradeoffs in plain terms. |
| Wifi Router | `wifi-router` / `__wifi-router.md` | true | Home-network buying guide; spec numbers mentioned but not required to act on. |
| What I Look For in Wireless Earphones | `what-i-look-for-in-wireless-earphones` / `what-i-look-for-in-wireless-earphones.md` | false | Personal buying checklist; Bluetooth/open-ear terms stay accessible. |
| 2nd Cellphone | `second-cellphone` / `__second-cellphone.md` | true | Security hygiene tip (backup phone for banking); practical, not technical. |
| Shopee Tips | `shopee-tips` / `__shopee-tips.md` | true | Philippines marketplace habits; local context helps but isn’t mandatory. |
| Stuart McGill's Big Three Low Back Exercises | `mcgill-big-three` / `__mcgill-big-three.md` | true | Three exercises with form cues; mild anatomy/fitness vocabulary. |
| How to Create a Good Habit | `how-to-create-a-good-habit` / `__how-to-create-a-good-habit.md` | true | James Clear four-laws summary; framework is simple and well signposted. |
| Why I Still Keep a Desktop or Laptop | `why-keep-desktop-laptop` / `why-keep-desktop-laptop.md` | false | Opinion piece with light tech terms (RAM, Hugo build) but no steps to follow. |
| One Sheet, Eight Pages | `one-page-zine` / `one-page-zine.md` | false | Papercraft/how-to for 1-page zines; mild print/layout vocabulary. |
| RSS Feeds - What I Wish I'd Done Sooner | `getting-started-with-rss-feeds` / `getting-started-with-rss-feeds.md` | false | Beginner RSS guide; app picks and folder advice, no technical pipeline. |
| Why I Still Run My Own Website | `why-run-your-own-website` / `why-run-your-own-website.md` | false | Philosophy of owning your site; platform-risk examples, not a build guide. |
| Sustainable Online Publishing | `sustainable-online-publishing` / `sustainable-online-publishing.md` | false | Habit/ownership argument with a short starter checklist; Hugo mentioned lightly. |
| Password Managers | `password-managers` / `__password-managers.md` | true | Security primer with comparison table; assumes basic “password reuse” awareness. |
| Kumon for the Reps. Math Games at Home | `grade-1-better-than-kumon` / `__grade-1-better-than-kumon.md` | true | Long parenting essay but written in plain voice; no technical workflow. |

---

## Tier 3 — Moderate
*Domain framing, comparisons, or density that rewards some background.*

| Title | Slug / filename | Draft | Justification |
|---|---|---|---|
| Alex Eala Pro Career Tracker | `alex-eala-pro-career-tracker` / `alex-eala-pro-career-tracker.md` | false | Enormous reference log; tennis scoring/WTA tier jargon and link density raise the bar. |
| Why I Switched from Drupal to WordPress | `drupal-to-wordpress` / `drupal-to-wordpress.md` | false | CMS career narrative assumes CMS vocabulary (Gutenberg, ACF, Views, contrib). |
| Why I Focused on Open Source Technologies | `why-i-focused-on-open-source` / `why-i-focused-on-open-source.md` | false | FOSS philosophy woven with Hugo/WordPress/hosting context; conceptual but clearly written. |
| Static Site or CMS? How I Pick the Stack | `static-site-vs-cms` / `static-site-vs-cms.md` | false | Decision framework balancing static vs WordPress vs git-CMS; needs web-project literacy. |
| Jobs to Be Done Framework | `jobs-to-be-done-framework` / `__jobs-to-be-done-framework.md` | true | Product-strategy framework with causal questions; abstract but illustrated with examples. |
| The CRAFT Prompt Framework | `craft-prompt-framework` / `__craft-prompt-framework.md` | true | Structured AI prompting (Context/Role/Action/Format/Target); meta-prompts add moderate complexity. |
| Make AI Articles More Human | `humanize-ai-articles` / `__humanize-ai-articles.md` | true | AI-writing craft with perplexity/burstiness concepts plus editing pass expectations. |
| Retheming Hanamikoji as Duopoly | `hanamikoji-duopoly-retheme` / `hanamikoji-duopoly-retheme.md` | false | Board-game retheme map; much easier if you know *Hanamikoji*’s “I split, you choose” mechanic. |
| What Happens After Your Website Goes Live | `client-site-pass-off` / `__client-site-pass-off.md` | true | Freelance pass-off models (Git, retainer, CMS) for static sites; business + light dev context. |
| How I Quote Client Websites | `selling-static-sites` / `__selling-static-sites.md` | true | Freelance quoting with Hugo/Cloudflare line items; client-work + static-stack assumptions. |
| Private Notes vs Hugo | `obsidian-vs-hugo` / `__obsidian-vs-hugo.md` | true | PKM workflow design (garage vs garden); short but needs notes/publishing mental model. |
| Markdown Formatting | `markdown-formatting` / `__markdown-formatting.md` | true | Writer-facing structure guide; easy for bloggers, moderate if Markdown/TOC is new. |
| Markdown Deep Dive | `markdown-deep-dive` / `__markdown-deep-dive.md` | true | Flavor differences, YAML frontmatter, renderer tables — publishing-stack literacy required. |
| Why I Love the Inter Font | `inter-font` / `__inter-font.md` | true | Typography/web-design choices (weights, self-hosting, Tailwind tokens); design vocabulary assumed. |
| Personal Immune Booster Supplements | `immune-booster-supplements` / `__immune-booster-supplements.md` | true | Supplement stack with research links; reader must weigh medical disclaimers and mixed evidence. |

---

## Tier 4 — Hard
*Developer-oriented; multi-step workflows or Hugo/build-pipeline knowledge expected.*

| Title | Slug / filename | Draft | Justification |
|---|---|---|---|
| Consistent AI Output for WordPress Builds | `consistent-ai-output-wordpress-builds` / `consistent-ai-output-wordpress-builds.md` | false | Four-step AI dev workflow (helpers, examples, rules doc) for WordPress/Cursor builds. |
| How I Built JoRap Notes | `how-i-built-jorap-notes` / `how-i-built-jorap-notes.md` | false | Full Hugo → GitHub → Cloudflare migration walkthrough with PATH, version pins, deploy debugging. |
| HugoPlate: The Theme Quietly Powering This Site | `hugoplate-theme-review` / `hugoplate-theme-review.md` | false | Long theme deep-dive: Tailwind v4, folder map, shortcodes, upgrade pain — dense for non-Hugo readers. |
| Hugo Static Site Generator | `hugo-static-site-generator` / `__hugo-static-site-generator.md` | true | SSG architecture, JAMstack tradeoffs, when-not-to-use — assumes web-stack comparison literacy. |
| Hugo Go Templates | `hugo-go-templates` / `__hugo-go-templates.md` | true | Go template syntax, partials, layout lookup; code snippets and theme-debug mindset required. |
| Hugo Page Bundles | `hugo-page-bundles` / `__hugo-page-bundles.md` | true | Content architecture (leaf/branch/headless bundles) with folder layouts and relative asset paths. |
| Hugo Shortcodes Deep Dive | `hugo-shortcodes` / `__hugo-shortcodes.md` | true | Custom shortcode anatomy, Go template params, delimiter footguns — hands-on Hugo templating. |
| Native Hugo Taxonomies | `native-hugo-taxonomies` / `__native-hugo-taxonomies.md` | true | Taxonomy config, template paths, custom taxonomy in `hugo.toml`; site-structure engineering. |
| Tailwind CSS v4.0 | `tailwind-css-v4` / `__tailwind-css-v4.md` | true | v3→v4 migration (`@theme`, Oxide, PostCSS/Vite coupling with Hugo assets). |
| Sveltia CMS | `sveltia-cms` / `__sveltia-cms.md` | true | Git-backed CMS setup (OAuth, schemas, staging) bridging static sites and non-technical editors. |

---

## Tier 5 — Expert
*Dense system documentation; many subsystems in one read.*

| Title | Slug / filename | Draft | Justification |
|---|---|---|---|
| What JoRap Notes Actually Does | `jorap-notes-features` / `__jorap-notes-features.md` | true | Exhaustive garden feature catalog (atomic frontmatter schema, typed relationships, PixiJS graph, OKF export, lint/review tooling) — assumes Hugo + PKM + agent-export fluency. |

---

## Summary stats

| Tier | Label | Count | Published | Draft |
|---|---|---:|---:|---:|
| 1 | Effortless | 13 | 6 | 7 |
| 2 | Easy | 19 | 10 | 9 |
| 3 | Moderate | 15 | 6 | 9 |
| 4 | Hard | 10 | 3 | 7 |
| 5 | Expert | 1 | 0 | 1 |
| **Total** | | **58** | **25** | **33** |

**Pattern:** Published posts cluster in Tiers 1–3 (personal essays, recipes, gear, opinions, guides). Draft Hugo/PKM/developer posts dominate Tiers 4–5. JoRap’s plain voice consistently lowers *perceived* difficulty versus raw topic complexity — especially on Tier 3–4 web-dev posts.
